problems.title: Hello, World!
problems.description: Write Python code to print 'Hello, World!' to the console.
problems.hint: Use the print() function with a string argument.
problems.explanation: print() outputs text to the console. Strings are enclosed in quotes.

problems.title: Variables and f-strings
problems.description: Use a variable and an f-string to print a personalized greeting.
problems.explanation: f-strings let you embed variables directly inside string literals using curly braces.

problems.title: Check a Variable's Type
problems.description: Print the data type of the variable 'value' using the type() function.
problems.explanation: type() returns the data type of any Python object.

problems.title: Create a List
problems.description: Print a list containing the numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, and 50.
problems.hint: Create a list with square brackets: [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
problems.explanation: Lists are ordered, mutable collections created with square brackets.

problems.title: Access List Elements
problems.description: Print the first and last elements of the 'fruits' list, each on its own line.
problems.hint: Use fruits[0] for the first element and fruits[-1] for the last.
problems.explanation: Python uses 0-based indexing. Negative indices count from the end of the list.

problems.title: List Append and Sort
problems.description: Append the number 9 to the list, sort it, then print the result.
problems.hint: Use .append(9) to add an item, then .sort() to sort in place.
problems.explanation: append() adds an element to the end of a list. sort() arranges elements in ascending order.

problems.title: Sum a List
problems.description: Calculate and print the sum of the 'prices' list, rounded to 2 decimal places.
problems.hint: Use round(sum(prices), 2) to avoid floating-point precision issues.
problems.explanation: sum() adds all elements of an iterable. round() controls decimal places.

problems.title: Create and Access a Dictionary
problems.description: Print the 'name' value from the 'person' dictionary.
problems.explanation: Dictionaries store key-value pairs. Access values by their key inside square brackets.

problems.title: Add a Key-Value Pair
problems.description: Add a 'history' key with value 78 to the scores dict, then print it.
problems.hint: Assign to a new key: scores['history'] = 78
problems.explanation: You can add new key-value pairs to a dictionary by simple assignment to a new key.

problems.title: Sum Dictionary Values
problems.description: Print the sum of all values in the 'inventory' dictionary.
problems.hint: Use sum(inventory.values()) to sum all values.
problems.explanation: dict.values() returns a view of all values. sum() adds them up.

problems.title: Even or Odd
problems.description: Print 'even' if the number is even, or 'odd' if it is odd.
problems.hint: Use the modulo operator % to check divisibility by 2.
problems.explanation: The modulo operator % returns the remainder. If num % 2 == 0, the number is even.

problems.title: Grade Letter
problems.description: Print the letter grade based on the score: A (90+), B (80-89), C (70-79), or D (<70).
problems.hint: Use if/elif/else to check ranges from highest to lowest.
problems.explanation: elif chains allow multiple conditions. The first true branch executes.

problems.title: Find the Maximum of Three Numbers
problems.description: Print the largest of the three numbers a, b, and c.
problems.explanation: max() returns the largest item in an iterable or among arguments.

problems.title: For Loop - Print Each Color
problems.description: Print each color from the list on its own line using a for loop.
problems.hint: Use 'for c in colors:' and print each color inside the loop.
problems.explanation: A for loop iterates over each element of a sequence, executing the body for each item.

problems.title: Sum Numbers 1 to 10
problems.description: Print the sum of all integers from 1 to 10 inclusive.
problems.explanation: range(1, 11) generates numbers 1 through 10. sum() adds them all at once.

problems.title: Count Down with While
problems.description: Print numbers from n down to 1, each on its own line, using a while loop.
problems.hint: Decrement n inside the loop with n -= 1.
problems.explanation: A while loop repeats as long as its condition is true. n -= 1 decrements the counter.

problems.title: Add Two Numbers
problems.description: Define a function that adds two numbers, call it with 3 and 5, and print the result.
problems.hint: Define 'def add(a, b): return a + b' then call and print.
problems.explanation: Functions are defined with def. return sends a value back to the caller.

problems.title: Check Palindrome
problems.description: Check if the word 'radar' reads the same forwards and backwards. Print True or False.
problems.hint: Compare word with word[::-1] using the == operator.
problems.explanation: A palindrome reads the same forward and backward. [::-1] reverses the string.

problems.title: Function with Default Argument
problems.description: Define a function greet() with a default parameter, and call it twice — once with no arguments and once with 'Python'.
problems.hint: Define def greet(name='World'): and use an f-string inside.
problems.explanation: Default argument values are used when the caller omits that argument.

problems.title: Variable Number of Arguments
problems.description: Define a function sum_all that accepts any number of arguments using *args and returns their sum.
problems.hint: Use *args in the function definition to capture all positional arguments.
problems.explanation: *args packs extra positional arguments into a tuple inside the function.

problems.title: Convert to Uppercase
problems.description: Convert the given text to uppercase and print it.
problems.explanation: String methods like .upper() return a new string without modifying the original.

problems.title: Split and Join a String
problems.description: Split the sentence into words and join them with hyphens.
problems.hint: First .split() the string, then '-'.join() the resulting list.
problems.explanation: .split() breaks a string into a list of words. '-'.join() merges them with hyphens.

problems.title: Count Characters Excluding Spaces
problems.description: Count and print the number of characters in the phrase, ignoring spaces.
problems.hint: Use .replace(' ', '') to remove spaces, then len() to count.
problems.explanation: len() returns the number of characters. Replacing spaces with empty string removes them before counting.

problems.title: List of Squares
problems.description: Use a list comprehension to create a list of squares for numbers 1 through 5.
problems.explanation: List comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists by applying an expression to each item.

problems.title: Filter Even Numbers
problems.description: Use a list comprehension with a condition to print only the even numbers.
problems.hint: Use [x for x in nums if x % 2 == 0].
problems.explanation: You can add an if clause at the end of a comprehension to filter elements.
problems.solution: print([x for x in nums if x % 2 == 0])

problems.title: Sort Words by Length
problems.description: Sort the list of words by their length (shortest first) and print the result.
problems.explanation: sorted() returns a new sorted list. The key parameter specifies a function to compute sort order.

problems.title: Convert Celsius to Fahrenheit
problems.description: Use map() with a lambda to convert each Celsius temperature to Fahrenheit, then print the list.
problems.explanation: map() applies a function to every item in an iterable. wrap in list() to materialize the result.

problems.title: Filter Numbers Greater Than Threshold
problems.description: Use filter() with a lambda to keep only numbers greater than the threshold, then print as a list.
problems.hint: Use list(filter(lambda x: x > threshold, nums)).
problems.explanation: filter() keeps items for which the function returns True. wrap in list() to materialize the result.

problems.title: Try/Except - Safe Division
problems.description: Use a try/except block to safely divide a by b. Print 'Error' if division fails.
problems.hint: Wrap print(a / b) in try, and catch all exceptions with except:.
problems.explanation: try/except catches runtime errors. When dividing by zero, a ZeroDivisionError is raised.

problems.title: List Slicing
problems.description: Use list slicing to print elements from index 2 up to (but not including) index 5.
problems.hint: Use alpha[2:5] to slice from index 2 to 4.
problems.explanation: List slicing uses start:end notation. The start index is inclusive, end is exclusive.

problems.title: Create a DataFrame
problems.description: Create a pandas DataFrame with Name and Age columns and print it.
problems.explanation: A DataFrame is a 2D labeled data structure. Columns are created from dictionary keys.

problems.title: DataFrame Shape
problems.description: Print the shape (number of rows, number of columns) of the DataFrame.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'x': [1,2,3,4], 'y': [5,6,7,8]})
problems.explanation: The shape attribute returns a tuple of (row_count, column_count).

problems.title: Series Mean
problems.description: Create a pandas Series and print its mean (average) value.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([10, 20, 30, 40, 50])
problems.explanation: The mean() method calculates the arithmetic average of Series values.

problems.title: Select a Column and Sum
problems.description: Print the sum of the 'Price' column from the DataFrame.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Product': ['A','B','C'], 'Price': [10, 20, 30]})
problems.explanation: Select a column with df['col'] and chain .sum() to add all values.

problems.title: Filter Rows by Condition
problems.description: Print only the rows where the Score is 90 or higher.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Name': ['Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave'], 'Score': [85, 92, 78, 95]})
problems.explanation: Boolean indexing filters rows where the condition evaluates to True.

problems.title: Add a New Column
problems.description: Add a 'Revenue' column calculated as Price * Qty, then print the Revenue Series.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Price': [10, 20, 30], 'Qty': [2, 3, 5]})
problems.hint: Assign df['Revenue'] = df['Price'] * df['Qty'], then print df['Revenue'].
problems.explanation: New columns are created by assigning to a new column name. Arithmetic is vectorized.

problems.title: Sort DataFrame by Column
problems.description: Sort the DataFrame by the 'Age' column in ascending order and print the result.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Name': ['Bob','Alice','Dave'], 'Age': [30, 25, 35]})
problems.explanation: sort_values() returns a new DataFrame sorted by the specified column.

problems.title: Multiple Condition Filter
problems.description: Print rows where City is 'NY' AND Sales is greater than 200.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'City': ['NY','LA','NY','SF'], 'Sales': [100, 250, 300, 150]})
problems.explanation: Use & (not 'and') for element-wise boolean operations. Each condition must be in parentheses.

problems.title: loc and iloc
problems.description: Print the value at row 'y', column 'A' using .loc, then the value at row 1, column 1 using .iloc.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1,2,3], 'B': [4,5,6]}, index=['x','y','z'])
problems.explanation: .loc uses label-based indexing. .iloc uses integer-position-based indexing.

problems.title: Rename a Column
problems.description: Rename the column 'old' to 'new' and print the DataFrame.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'old': [1, 2, 3]})
problems.explanation: rename() creates a new DataFrame with updated column names. The original is unchanged unless inplace=True.

problems.title: GroupBy Mean
problems.description: Group by 'Category' and print the mean 'Value' for each group.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Category': ['A','B','A','B'], 'Value': [10, 20, 30, 40]})
problems.expected_output:
Category
A    20.0
B    30.0
Name: Value, dtype: float64
problems.explanation: groupby() splits data into groups. ['Value'].mean() computes the average per group.

problems.title: GroupBy Sum
problems.description: Group by 'Dept' and print the sum of 'Revenue' for each department.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Dept': ['Sales','IT','Sales','IT'], 'Revenue': [100, 200, 300, 400]})
problems.expected_output:
Dept
IT       600
Sales    400
Name: Revenue, dtype: int64
problems.explanation: .sum() adds all values in each group. The result is a Series with a group label index.

problems.title: Value Counts
problems.description: Print the frequency count of each unique value in the Series.
problems.expected_output:
red      3
blue     2
green    1
Name: count, dtype: int64
problems.explanation: value_counts() returns a Series with counts of unique values, sorted descending by default.

problems.title: Apply Function to Column
problems.description: Convert Celsius temperatures to Fahrenheit using .apply() with a lambda, then print the result.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'Temp_C': [0, 10, 20, 30]})
problems.hint: Use df['Temp_C'].apply(lambda c: c * 9/5 + 32) and assign to df['Temp_F'].
problems.explanation: .apply() applies a function element-wise to each value in a Series or column.

problems.title: Drop Duplicates
problems.description: Drop duplicate rows from the DataFrame and print the result.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'City': ['NY','LA','NY','SF','LA']})
problems.explanation: drop_duplicates() removes rows where all column values are identical, keeping the first occurrence.

problems.title: Merge Two DataFrames
problems.description: Perform an inner merge of two DataFrames on the 'ID' column and print the result.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df1 = pd.DataFrame({'ID': [1,2,3], 'Name': ['A','B','C']})
df2 = pd.DataFrame({'ID': [1,2,4], 'Score': [85, 92, 78]})
problems.explanation: merge() combines DataFrames based on a common column. Inner join keeps only matching keys.

problems.title: Handle Missing Values
problems.description: Fill all missing values (NaN) with 0 and print the DataFrame.
problems.setup_code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, None, 3], 'B': [None, 5, 6]})
problems.explanation: fillna() replaces NaN or None values with a specified value.

problems.title: Create a NumPy Array
problems.description: Create a NumPy array with values 1 through 5 and print it.
problems.explanation: np.array() creates a NumPy array. Arrays print without commas, unlike Python lists.

problems.title: NumPy Mean and Sum
problems.description: Print the mean and the sum of a NumPy array, each on its own line.
problems.setup_code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([10, 20, 30, 40, 50])
problems.explanation: NumPy arrays have built-in methods for common statistical operations like mean() and sum().

problems.title: NumPy Reshape
problems.description: Reshape a 1D NumPy array of 6 elements into a 2x3 2D array and print it.
problems.setup_code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6])
problems.explanation: reshape() changes the dimensions of an array without changing its data. The total element count must remain the same.
